desc: Cuenca sits in Ecuador's southern Andes, blending UNESCO-era churches, café culture, and a relaxed high-altitude pace that suits culture-first travelers who also want easy access to Cajas páramo hikes.
hls: UNESCO-listed historic core with cathedral domes, plazas, and riverwalk districts
Strong museum, artisan, and food-market culture in a compact, easy-to-walk center
Fast access to Cajas National Park lagoons, páramo roads, and acclimatized day hikes
Mild daytime temperatures that reward slow city wandering more than rushed sightseeing

todo.name: Old Town + New Cathedral rooftop circuit
todo.desc: Link Plaza Calderón, the blue-domed Catedral Nueva, and El Barranco viewpoints in one walk.

todo.name: Pumapungo Museum and Inca ruins
todo.desc: Pair the ethnography galleries with the adjacent archaeological zone and riverside gardens.

todo.name: Cajas National Park Toreadora trails
todo.desc: Start from Laguna Toreadora for acclimatized páramo hiking among lakes and Polylepis forest.

todo.name: Chordeleg and Gualaceo craft day
todo.desc: Do a day loop to workshops known for silver filigree, weaving, and Panama-hat finishing.

todo.name: Mercado 10 de Agosto food crawl
todo.desc: Sample mote dishes, jugos, and hornado counters with strong local lunchtime trade.

todo.name: Tomebamba and Yanuncay river promenade
todo.desc: Walk both rivers at golden hour for terrace cafés and colonial facades.

wifi.note: Good fiber availability in central neighborhoods, coworking spots, and serviced apartments, though older colonial buildings can have room-to-room variation.

prac.fltNote: From the UK/Ireland, the most reliable long-haul pattern is Europe/US to Quito (UIO) or Guayaquil (GYE), then a short domestic flight to Cuenca (CUE); overland transfers from GYE/UIO are possible but weather and mountain roads can add delay risk, especially in wetter months and holiday periods.

airports.name: José Joaquín de Olmedo International Airport (Guayaquil)

prac.currency: United States dollar (USD); cards are commonly accepted in hotels, cafés, and modern restaurants, but cash remains useful for taxis, markets, buses, and smaller family-run places.
prac.lang: Spanish is the primary language; Kichwa is also spoken in some communities.
prac.tz: Ecuador Time (ECT, UTC-5) on the mainland; no daylight saving time.
prac.visa: Ecuador generally grants many tourists up to 90 days visa-free, but passport validity, onward-ticket proof, and any later Galápagos transit plans can affect what documents you need, so verify the current rule set for your passport.
alerts: Cuenca sits above 2,500 m: schedule a lighter first 24 hours to reduce altitude-related fatigue.
On the Cajas/El Cajas corridor, fog, cold rain, and sudden visibility drops are common in afternoons—start mountain trips early.
Road links toward Guayaquil can see landslide or closure disruptions in wetter spells; avoid last-minute same-day international onward connections.

sweet: May-Sep for drier mornings and stronger hiking reliability, with Oct-Nov good for city culture trips around Independence festivities and fewer peak-season prices.
